purpose: Build evidence-first, information-dense Markdown source corpora before authoring or revising QA rows.

canonical_markdown_source_corpus.description: Cleaned, information-dense Markdown preserving the substantive knowledge and instructional structure of each accessible source, with page-level provenance. This is a first-class training artifact, not disposable QA-generation output.

derived_manual_qa.description: Natural questions that a rope learner might plausibly ask, with complete factual answers derived from the canonical Markdown and checked manually against the cited source page.

knowledge_taxonomy.goal: Maximize durable rope-bondage, shibari, and kinbaku knowledge rather than URL, title, navigation, commerce, or archive trivia.

categories.coverage: Traditions, schools, lineages, influential practitioners, documented contributions, relationships, and historical development; retain biography only when it explains durable rope knowledge or lineage.
Tie families, knots, hitches, bends, frictions, lockoffs, extensions, cinches, construction sequences, dressing, verification, uses, limitations, and failure modes.

Upline techniques, suspension mechanics, redirects, lowering, transitions, hardpoint types, tree rigging, indoor structures, ceiling-tension systems, portable frames, rig evaluation, and rejection criteria.
Nerves, circulation, joints, breathing, placement hazards, warning signs, monitoring, injury prevention, recovery boundaries, and appropriately attributed medical uncertainty.
Fiber and construction, diameter, stretch, strength, treatment, washing, conditioning, inspection, retirement, contamination, storage, and hygiene.
Tension, vectors, load paths, force multiplication, dynamic loading, redundancy, anchors, connectors, rings, pulleys, plates, frames, and hardware compatibility.
Negotiation, consent, communication loss, monitoring, safewords and alternatives, boundaries, aftercare, incident response, accountability, and partner evaluation.
Body awareness, active positioning, communication under stress, differentiating expected sensation from injury signals, endurance limits, suspension readiness, and informed self-advocacy.
Harness and pattern design, wrap and cinch placement, stability, body mechanics, transitions, partial suspension, reproducibility, and adaptation to different bodies.
Cutting and lowering plans, stuck systems, fainting, acute nerve or circulation symptoms, suspension emergencies, emergency equipment, and escalation to professional care.
Feeding, dressing, tension consistency, speed and control, rope extension, tail management, reversing direction, placement, flow, and troubleshooting.
Prerequisites, progressions, drills, tutorial and instructor evaluation, supervised practice, assessment, debugging, and common learner failure modes.
Body diversity, mobility limitations, disability, sensory needs, self-tying, communication accommodations, and lower-risk adaptations.
Composition, line placement, symmetry and asymmetry, movement, photography, stage practice, performance planning, and performer communication.
Japanese and Western terminology, school-specific usage, translation limits, cultural context, attribution, contested claims, and avoidance of false universal definitions.

queue.scope: Former article bodies and clearly verified successor or archived copies; remove all active title-to-URL trivia.
All same-domain substantive educational pages.
All substantive instructional, safety, terminology, practice, and rope-care pages.
queue.discarded_attempt: rope365_corpus was stopped before acceptance after a broad repository search exposed evaluation snippets

queue.scope: The entire accessible site inventory: every substantive instructional, safety, technique, equipment, anatomy, consent, floor-rope, suspension, upline, hardpoint, rigging, cultural, and reference page. Include all durable rope-bondage facts in the canonical Markdown; exclude only non-content shells, duplicates, inaccessible material, and genuinely unrelated pages with explicit reasons.
Publicly accessible substantive floor-rope instruction; mark gated media without inference.
Public course descriptions and substantive educational text; do not bypass membership gates.
Public tutorial text and factual media metadata; do not bypass purchase gates.
Dense transcript-derived technical guide with timestamps and visual-only limitations.
Every available playlist video, with per-video transcript facts and timestamps.
Dense transcript-derived comparison and technique guide with timestamps.
Bondage-relevant rigging-plate specifications, intended uses, constraints, and safety text only.
Portable suspension-frame specifications, setup, constraints, compatibility, and safety text.
A-FRAME product and official manual facts relevant to suspension-frame decisions and safe use.
Suspension-grade bamboo specifications, preparation, limitations, and care only.
Stable manufacturer/product facts relevant to emergency rope cutting; exclude reviews and volatile commerce data.
Stable bondage-rope materials, construction, treatment, sizing, and care facts; exclude reviews and volatile listings.
Natural-fiber rope materials, construction, treatment, selection, maintenance, and safety facts.
Only synthetic-upline specifications, material properties, relevant custom-order facts, and care; never crawl the general catalog.
Stable synthetic bondage-rope material, construction, sizing, handling, and care facts only.
Stable nylon bondage-rope material, construction, sizing, handling, and care facts only.
Stable convention policies, education, safety, conduct, accessibility, and attendee guidance; exclude dates and prices unless explicitly time-stamped.
Stable convention policies, education, safety, conduct, accessibility, and attendee guidance; exclude dates and prices unless explicitly time-stamped.
Stable venue/community policies, consent, conduct, accessibility, and participation guidance; do not mirror the changing calendar.
Stable group purpose, education format, participation, consent, safety, and conduct information.
Stable educational philosophy, technique/style, safety, prerequisites, and class-scope information.

Inventory the same-domain journal and retain substantive History, Translations, Interviews, Articles, and text-bearing How To pages. Exclude announcements, event notices, galleries without explanatory prose, reviews that contain only commerce metadata, comments, author/category navigation, account/cart pages, and image- or video-only detail. Preserve author, publication date, translator, cited-source, uncertainty, and first-person/opinion attribution.

Crawl substantive article-namespace pages for people, organizations, publications, chronology, terminology, and technique-history records. Exclude edit/history/talk pages, user pages, category and index shells, event listings with no durable context, venue directories, navigation, and media-only entries. Keep citations, aliases, uncertainty markers, original Japanese names, and source-specific claims; do not treat wiki synthesis as primary evidence when a cited primary source is available.

Retain substantive historical essays, bibliographic research, and complete public translations with translator, editor, source publication, date, and uncertainty attribution. Deduplicate current-domain and legacy WordPress copies. Exclude donation prompts, comments, navigation, tag/date archives, book entries containing only cover or ISBN metadata, and image-only scans; never infer untranslated or obscured text from images.

Inventory the English educational webbook first and add untranslated unique Italian material only when it adds facts. Include substantive safety, consent/negotiation, rope and technique, history, glossary, interview, bottoming, and suspension/upline pages. Deduplicate language translations and repeated series navigation. Exclude galleries, events, cookie text, comments, tag/date archives, and video-only technique beyond explicit surrounding prose; flag potentially medical claims for attribution and later review.

Create only a quotation-light, attributed factual digest under ordinary copyright. Build a claim-to-citation matrix and independently verify every cited source identity and whether it actually supports the associated statement. Preserve source-labeled community observations about negotiation, communication, bottom agency, accessibility, emergency planning, restraint removal, and migration from Remedial Ropes. Quarantine exact onset or recovery timings, the two-finger rule, capillary-refill heuristics, harness-hang claims, drug or supplement advice including ibuprofen and vitamin B12, ice or heat treatment, dosing, exercise, prognosis, and all other medical advice unless corroborated by a current authoritative source; direct care guidance must defer to qualified clinicians and emergency services. Exclude copied prose, images, events, class sales/logistics, performance galleries, comments, and unrelated kink topics; deduplicate migrated copies.

Inventory all public substantive safety, preparation, equipment, upline, pulley, harness/body-mechanics, emergency, nerve, transition, and self-suspension technique pages. Preserve first-person incident attribution and warnings. Exclude gallery-only pages, biography, link directories, comments, and external product availability; mark visual-only technique steps and do not present self-suspension as safely performed alone.

Extract only the official public Rope Bottom Guide PDF and any official version/translation metadata. Preserve author, version, headings, cautions, checklists, and distinctions between personal variation and general guidance. Do not infer content from diagrams, reproduce photography, or ingest mirrors and book/store listings.

Include substantive English articles plus unique Finnish material on rope risks, nerves, negotiation, consent, safer-space practice, reporting, intervention, and accessibility. Deduplicate translations. Exclude calendars, event descriptions, current staff lists, contact details, venue promotions, and repeated navigation; retain dates and authors because community policies and safety guidance can change.

Crawl substantive Education, Community & Culture, accessibility, code, consent/etiquette, nerve-safety, bottoming, history, and performance-planning pages. Exclude calendar, tickets, class schedules, store/catalog, testimonials, gallery-only pages, venue news, contact/location details, and generic promotional copy. Attribute pregnancy, biochemistry, and other health claims and require later safety review.

Allowlist only substantive rope-care, fiber/material, upline/loading, safety, consent/partner selection, and accessibility/body-diversity education pages. Exclude products, collections, prices, stock, reviews, shipping, sales, customer stories, newsletters, travel posts, repetitive Shopify chrome, and unsupported marketing claims. Keep all material and strength claims attributed to the vendor or named guest author.

Crawl only public/free articles, calculators, quizzes, and reports directly relevant to human suspension: forces/vectors, multi-point anchors, redirects, friction, mechanical advantage, pulleys, dynamic loading, rope abrasion, connectors, tripods, redundancy, and rescue/lowering. Do not access member content or bypass gates. Exclude events, comments, newsletters, unrelated industrial scenarios, and product availability. Preserve professional-rigging context and never convert industrial/rescue values into bondage-specific guarantees without explicit source support.

Include substantive public pages on terminology, history, declared lineage/influences, semenawa philosophy, Elements methodology, somatics/body scans for bottoms, and explicit educational prerequisites/safety. Exclude bookings, schedules, galleries, testimonials, studio promotion, shop links, podcast embeds without transcripts, and thin workshop advertisements. Label history and philosophy as the authors' perspective and deduplicate German/English translations.

Allowlist only public rope anatomy, nerve assessment, restraint removal/cutting, and medical-emergency material directly applicable to rope bondage. Preserve author credentials, citations, disclaimers, and tool-specific limitations. Exclude waterboarding, choking/breath play, blood play, rape play, rough body play, caning, class logistics, testimonials, comments, and unrelated reference PDFs.

Extract only the official 2023 MI Ropes Rope Safety PDF and official version/author metadata. Exclude fire-cupping and other unrelated handouts, event/conference information, and the resource URL list as training trivia. Preserve warnings and distinguish the handout's recommendations from cross-source consensus; do not infer visual-only details.

Review and allowlist only complete public journal articles containing substantive rope safety, consent, bottoming, pedagogy, or performance knowledge. Exclude tuition, bookings, services, store, event promotion, gallery, portfolio, testimonials, city SEO, and generic inspirational copy. Preserve named author and disclaimer; attribute health claims and exclude any unsupported medical or physiological generalization during extraction.

Allowlist public Professional technical tips and test reports directly relevant to anchors, force paths, redirects, hauling/lowering, rescue, rope/device interaction, and dynamic or slack-load behavior. Exclude catalog, store, dealer, marketing, and unrelated sport pages. Preserve exact device, rope, mass, geometry, test, date, and professional-training conditions; never generalize a product result or certification into a bondage guarantee.

Allowlist only complete public Knowledge articles and test reports on pulley mechanics, anchor loads, slings, connectors, rope behavior, friction, edges, dynamic/slack loading, and rescue systems. Exclude storefront, collections, prices, stock, reviews, newsletters, and product-selection marketing. Preserve author, test setup, geometry, material, units, sample size, uncertainty, and failure context; do not convert industrial tests into human-suspension certification.

Capture only freely public official Safety Guides 1 and 3 sections relevant to human-support tree condition, anchor selection/testing, system configuration, foreseeable or peak loading, component compatibility, slack/fall minimization, lowering, inspection, and aerial rescue. Preserve guide title, date, version, checklist status, and stated training limits. Exclude paid Technical Guides, unavailable drafts, cutting/dismantling logistics except necessary mechanics, and visual-only inference; never treat a tree, species, or configuration as certified for bondage.

Allowlist only named author-hosted research papers, HSE project outputs, and technical presentations on measured anchor, redirect, friction, fall/dynamic, rope, and load-path forces or risk controls. Exclude consulting promotion, software marketing, client information, unrelated wind/tree-statics research, and cutting logistics. Preserve funding, authors, date, experiment, units, apparatus, arboriculture context, and limitations; no tree or bondage certification may be inferred.

Capture only durable risk-management, competence, independent-anchor/system, inspection, communication, fall-prevention, and rescue-planning principles from the official public manual. Preserve the 2008 edition, circus context, country/legal references, and translations; exclude or quarantine jurisdiction-specific thresholds, superseded standards, and dated product/equipment assertions. Do not treat FEDEC guidance as structural or bondage certification.

Capture this checklist and stable provenance only. Retain UK, performer-flying, inspection-period, legal, and collaboration context. Extract durable questions about declared load, venue evidence, competent installation, inspection, full load path, dynamic loading, clear air, automation failsafes, and rescue; do not infer DIY installation instructions or capacity and do not treat it as bondage certification.

Allowlist only official engineering-guide sections on anchor qualification, cracked/uncracked concrete or masonry, steel attachment, edge/spacing, embedment, installation, drilling/cleaning, adhesive curing, corrosion, failure modes, and combined loading. Exclude catalog, products, selectors, prices, availability, case-study marketing, and volatile approvals. Preserve edition, code, base material, product/system, installation, and test context. Require a qualified structural engineer; no capacity may be transferred to an unassessed ceiling or human suspension.

Allowlist only complete text-bearing search/rescue or climbing pages for Prusik and Klemheist hitches, Munter/Mule/Super Munter lockoffs, tensionless hitch, figure-eight loops/bends, butterfly, double fisherman, water knot, and girth hitch where relevant. Preserve warnings, rope/cord diameter, material, direction, tail, dressing, carabiner, and load context. Do not copy or reconstruct animations/images, and exclude fishing, boating, decorative knots, apps, ads, store links, and visual-only steps; no knot is certified here for bondage.

Capture durable, source-attributed mechanics and procedures on anchor systems, deviations, knots/lockoffs, friction, mechanical advantage, rope/equipment inspection, lowering/raising, communication, rescue planning, and training controls. Preserve the 2004 U.S. industrial context and flag superseded regulations, standards, product guidance, forms, and contact/resource lists. Exclude job-administration templates and never treat industrial guidance as bondage-specific certification.

Capture a concise factual digest of direct interview testimony only, with speaker, interviewer, date, and first-person status attached. Include declared influences, practice development, aesthetics, performance, trust, role, and explicitly framed safety observations. Do not reproduce extended quotations or photography; exclude inline Wikipedia/external summaries, broad unsourced history, therapeutic/endorphin claims, and unrelated magazine chrome.

Create a transformed factual digest of methodology, clearly identified interview testimony, field observations, communication/negotiation, participant experience, material relationship, and performance analysis. Preserve author, undergraduate status, date, bibliography, speaker, and evidence type. Do not republish chapters or images; exclude or separately review secondary origin history, medical/physiological claims, and unsafe tie specifics, and never universalize participant accounts.

Capture a concise, attributed digest of direct interview material on disabled/chronic-pain rope experience, representation, body-first positioning, access needs, communication, nonverbal stop signals, pacing, and adaptation principles. Do not reproduce extended quotations or images. Exclude store/newsletter/related-article chrome, generic lifestyle copy, unsupported therapeutic or physiotherapy claims, product recommendations, and medical generalizations.

Capture the CC BY 3.0 JATS from the EMBL-EBI Europe PMC API only, preserving title, authors, journal, publication date, DOI/PMCID/PMID, license, study design, population, measures, results, limitations, and evidence level. Bind PMCID PMC10294117, DOI 10.7759/cureus.39588, and PMID 37384078 in the manifest. Clearly label self-reported associations rather than causal rules; retain subgroup denominators and uncertainty; do not generalize beyond the sampled population or turn observed injury locations into tying instructions. Exclude publisher chrome and do not access the Cureus or US PMC copies.

Capture the CC BY 4.0 JATS from the EMBL-EBI Europe PMC API only. Bind PMCID PMC10710713, DOI 10.1186/s13049-023-01164-z, and PMID 38071341 in the manifest. Preserve review methods, population/context, strength and limits of evidence, graded recommendations, terminology, dates, and citations. Distinguish harness suspension, occupational rescue, and experimental evidence from rope bondage; never present transfer as validated without direct evidence. Retain the review's rejection of unsupported blood-pooling and seated-recovery claims. Exclude publisher chrome and do not crawl Springer or US PMC endpoints.

Create a concise transformed factual digest of performer flying/aerial stunts, rigging, fall arrest, working at height, temporary structures, outdoor conditions, anchors, inspection, rehearsals, rescue, and competent-role definitions. Attribute Ontario and preserve issue/revision dates and legal jurisdiction. Do not reproduce the Crown text wholesale, treat regulatory language as universally applicable, or imply that entertainment guidance validates any bondage-specific system.

Inventory each substantive Consent Counts resource and record its exact title, author/organization, date/version, URL, and item-level license. Preserve CC BY-SA 4.0 educational material under its terms; create concise attributed factual digests of ordinary-copyright pages. Focus on explicit prior permission, negotiation, red flags, consent violations, sample organizational policy, response teams, reporting, confidentiality, trauma-informed response, and survey findings. Exclude fundraising, lobbying, membership, event, and generic site chrome; do not conflate community best practices with law or professional clinical advice.

Create an attributed, transformed digest of capacity and consent, body-first positioning, support, pressure distribution, surface friction and stability, sensation, grip, communication, pacing, and advance planning. Preserve author credentials and publication context, frame suggestions as individualized prompts rather than medical prescriptions, and cross-check safety statements. Exclude the solo-pulley scenario, shopping/product material, unrelated play suggestions, and any implication that disability or diagnosis alone determines capacity or technique.

Capture official GovInfo MODS metadata and source-attributed federal text on terminology, rope/hardware properties, knot efficiency, bend radius, slings, anchors, load paths, angles, friction, blocks, mechanical advantage, inspection, communication, planning, and rescue. Audit every credited image/table/excerpt and omit third-party expressive material unless separately licensed. Preserve forestry context, dates, units, assumptions, warnings, and cited standards; do not imply trail-work systems, ratings, knots, anchors, or equipment are automatically suitable for bondage or human suspension.

Create concise attributed factual digests of the two named bulletins: competent and qualified roles, design review, passive secondary protection, live/dynamic loading, abrasion and edges, inspection/retirement, rehearsal through full range, communication, exclusion zones, performer fit, emergency response, and rescue planning. Preserve publication dates, jurisdiction and entertainment context; do not copy diagrams or extended prose and do not imply the guidance certifies bondage or human-suspension systems.

Store bibliographic metadata and a concise factual digest limited to what the public abstract states: conference-abstract status, two cross-sectional survey sample sizes, reported injury ranges, nerve-symptom reporting, suspension association, and stated limitations. Do not reproduce the abstract, infer causation, calculate missing measures, generalize to all practitioners, or treat the preliminary report as equivalent to a peer-reviewed full study.

For noncommercial editorial use, create a concise attributed factual digest of the recommendation's revision date, tested rope context, named connections, situational selection, dressing, setting, tail, edge, load, and failure cautions. Preserve that it concerns kernmantle rescue ropes and ICAR TERCOM; do not copy diagrams or prose, teach it as bondage technique, transfer rescue results to natural-fiber rope or human suspension, or use expressively/commercially without written permission.

Capture the CC BY 4.0 article with complete attribution and license metadata, emphasizing study design, recruitment, sample, analytic method, limitations, and clearly attributed participant reports. Label all healing or therapeutic language as subjective experience or author interpretation; do not claim efficacy, recommend rope as treatment, omit adverse possibilities, or generalize ten interviews. Minimize memorization of long participant quotations and preserve cultural/terminological context.

Capture the resource hub, core-curriculum page, and the three named public Google documents through their anonymous public/export routes, recording exact document title, owner, retrieval date, export URL, visible share-with-attribution wording, and a snapshot hash. Preserve substantive history/terminology, rope selection and processing, emotional and physical safety, single/double columns, basic rigging, bottom advocacy, nerve material, accessibility, physical-preparation exercises, prerequisites, progression, and stated limits of internet-only learning. Treat the documents as living practitioner curriculum, not consensus: independently adjudicate or quarantine every anatomy, injury, circulation-time, exercise, load, and medical claim; retain the roughly-ten-bottom observational basis of the PT guide. Exclude schedules, ticketing, private lessons, images, contact details, and promotion; do not infer steps from embedded media.

Allowlist the writings index, Akechi Denki, Nureki Chimuo, Yukimura Haruki, Osada Eikichi, Oda Hisashi, Osada Steve, terminology essays, about, and training pages. Produce an original, quotation-light digest that records speaker, interviewer/author, interview or repost date, original publication when stated, Japanese name/term, first-person status, and uncertainty. Keep direct testimony separate from interviewer narration, promotion, recollection, and later synthesis; cross-check dates, lineage, hojojutsu, linguistic, anatomy, and safety claims against stronger sources and preserve disagreements. Exclude images, comments, dojo listings, student promotion, and extended prose; do not turn historical gender norms, injury claims, or master/disciple rhetoric into universal consent or technique rules.

Capture a concise attributed digest of the official profile's first-person artistic statement; declared learning from or relationships with Yumeko, Teru, Akechi Denki, Kanna, Yukimura Haruki, Chiharu Karino, Norio Sugiura, and other explicitly named influences; formation of Ichinawa-kai; and selected dated milestones that explain the development of his rope, photography, teaching, performance, or installation practice. Preserve Japanese terms such as shisho and onshi as the profile uses them and label all lineage as self-described. Exclude images, embedded video, galleries, current tours/events, exhaustive celebrity/commercial credits, purchase links, promotional superlatives, and volatile schedules; do not infer technique or safety from visual work.

Capture only the stable Japanese biography and a careful, source-labeled English factual digest of its dates, roles, named films/directors, technical-supervision work, workshops, instructional publications, writing, photography, stage practice, and bakutaku description. Record the site's January 2026 provisional status and distinguish biography claims from independently verified credits. Exclude images, galleries, event listings, work inquiries, social links, sales, forthcoming paid note content, exhaustive publicity, and external storefronts; do not translate promotional superlatives as neutral facts.

Allowlist only the substantive Studio Series 102, 201, 301, and 401 curriculum pages that are publicly accessible. Create a concise attributed pedagogy map preserving track order, prerequisites, intended competency, named technical topics, harness/body adaptation, uplines and lockoffs, line tension and force vectors, ground testing and go/no-go reasoning, nawajiri redistribution, bamboo pressure distribution, hashira, inversions, transitions, communication, and problem-solving emphasis. Treat descriptions as the studio's curriculum, not validated instructions; do not invent construction steps, certify any harness or hardpoint, or infer from images. Exclude class dates, tickets, membership, shop, services, staff promotion, venue details, and event listings.

On a fresh access-policy check, capture only the current public home and about pages, plus any restored topic/progression page reachable through ordinary public navigation without a 406, challenge, login, or workaround. Snapshot retrieval date and page status because the May 2026 rebuild is incomplete. Preserve the 101-401 prerequisites and conceptual progression, distinctions among practice/performance/play, deliberate practice, risk decisions, top/bottom partnership, stewardship, teaching limits, humility, and lineage acknowledgment as Rope Study's pedagogy. Exclude contact/social links, missing-page inference, obsolete cached lessons, and any claim that this outline itself teaches suspension technique or certifies competence.

Capture official MODS and only relevant federal text on wood structure, species and grade variability, moisture and duration effects, mechanical properties, fasteners and connector joints, edge and spacing variables, engineered wood, deterioration, inspection, and structural-analysis assumptions. Preserve chapter/page, units, test context, load direction and duration, species/grade/moisture conditions, uncertainty, and every warning. Audit and omit third-party credited expression unless separately cleared. This source does not certify any ceiling, beam, joist, connector, tree, hardpoint, bondage system, or human-suspension use; site-specific assessment belongs to a qualified structural engineer, and handbook values must never be converted into a do-it-yourself capacity guarantee.

Capture only the CC BY 4.0 JATS from the EMBL-EBI Europe PMC API and bind PMCID PMC8813685, DOI 10.1007/s00414-021-02674-0, and PMID 34383118 in the manifest. Preserve search method, inclusion criteria, 17-case publication-derived denominator, terminology, case-source provenance, uncertainty, selection and publication bias, lack of a population denominator, and inability to estimate incidence or causal risk. Distinguish consensual rope bondage from neck compression, erotic asphyxia, self-bondage, hanging, and other mechanisms; source-label the paper's recommendations and questionable correlations rather than treating them as settled. Exclude sensational narrative, identifiable case detail, instructional reconstruction, and unsupported generalization.

Create a quotation-light, fully attributed digest of Yukimura Haruki's first-person statements, Keisuke Higure's family testimony, Osada Steve's recollection, named practice concepts, terminology, artistic context, and kyū/license progression. Record speaker, author/interviewer, original and repost dates, publication context, and claim type. Preserve the unresolved 2006/2007 contradiction about Osada's formal-student date rather than reconciling it; cross-check all lineage, historical, linguistic, anatomy, and safety claims. Treat gendered, sexual, philosophical, and master/student statements as dated individual views, not present-day consent norms. Exclude images, video, extended quotations, schedules, fees, promotion, and technique inferred from media.

Allowlist only the four exact history-series articles dated 2015 and 2018. Produce an original quotation-light English synthesis attributed to Nawakiri Shin/小林縄霧; record article date, language, claim type, cited source, and uncertainty. Preserve useful leads about hojojutsu, Kabuki, ukiyo-e, ero-guro, Kitan Club contributors, censorship, publishing networks, and post-1950s technique change while explicitly separating visual analogy, practitioner interpretation, secondary scholarship, and corroborated chronology. Exclude images, scans, full translation, historical technique instruction, and extended prose. Quarantine the unsupported kannuki bruising-frequency claim, chest-band slippage claims, and all technique or safety generalizations unless corroborated by stronger evidence.

Create a concise quotation-light pedagogy map attributed to Willcat/Tension: declared educator background and limits, prerequisite and assessment structure, fundamentals, named knots/frictions and pattern concepts, negotiation, aftercare, debrief, tying and bottoming skill development, lab/integration stages, and the progression before uplines or load. Preserve the page's under-construction status and distinguish curriculum labels from validated instructions. Exclude unsupported history and linguistic claims, medical/anatomy guidance, pressure or body-reading claims, absolute safety statements such as appearance implying correctness, construction steps, prices, bookings, class promotion, images, and media-only detail.

Create only a quotation-light attributed digest of Bruce Argue's firsthand rope-maker observations about rope lay, strand imbalance, high-stranding, tangling, and handling behavior, cross-checked against stronger cordage references. Label demonstrations and incident interpretations as maker observations, not established general rules. Exclude copied prose and images, product and sales claims, tutorial construction steps, history or translation claims, suspension load and rope-size recommendations, nerve/anatomy claims, the assumed American Death Triangle analogy, gote safety assertions, uncorroborated breakage causation, prices, catalog, reviews, events, and all promotion.

Create a concise quotation-light pedagogy map preserving the sequence from beginner jams through Fundamentals, Progressions, Ascent, and Onward; class-count and 14-month pacing; floorwork-before-suspension principle; both-partners progression; prerequisites; partnered practice; communication, consent, body awareness, adaptation, reflection, and explicit non-ryu subjectivity. Treat every competence, good-versus-bad-pain, circulation, nerve-monitoring, troubleshooting, anatomy, and universal adaptation statement as the studio's practitioner curriculum rather than validated safety guidance. Exclude schedules, tickets, scholarships, contact details, teacher promotion, testimonials, images, shop, and event logistics; do not reconstruct class content or certify suspension competence.

Create a concise quotation-light curriculum architecture preserving Level 0-4 order, prerequisites, community responsibility, consent and mutual care, risk framing, knots/frictions, rope/body handling, body bamboo, harness structure, partner awareness, bottom education, partials, uplines, decision-making, in-air handling, static/dynamic suspension, adaptation, and lab/study-group roles. Keep class names only where they clarify progression. Treat anatomy, nerve safety, risk mitigation, suspendability, and competence as Temple's curriculum claims; do not invent construction steps, endorse a harness, or certify any learner, hardpoint, or suspension. Exclude events, dates, registration, rentals, location, contacts, policy duplication, images, and promotional language.

Create a concise original factual digest of the museum's Seiu Ito chronology: birth in 1882 and places, apprenticeship and sign-painting/theater exposure, newspaper illustration and criticism, period/custom research, development of seme-e in his mid-thirties, broader artistic production, death in 1961 at age 78, named collection provenance, and related bibliography. Preserve Japanese names/terms and distinguish curator description from later kinbaku interpretation. Do not copy exhibition prose, images, collection reproductions, captions, or the guest essay; do not label Ito the father of modern kinbaku, infer direct technique transmission, or convert depictions of coercion into consensual practice history without separate source-critical evidence. Exclude event logistics and prices.

Capture only the exact structured authority fields for NDL IDs 00023065, 00731050, and 034485143 through their public HTML or JSON-LD records; record retrieval date, record creation/update dates, source fields, and explicit attribution to Web NDL Authorities. Preserve Japanese script, katakana transcription, romanization, authorized-versus-variant or related-name status, personal-information caution, and the distinction between a person authority and the Kitan Club uniform title. Do not expand into bulk unrelated NDL data, external VIAF/Wikidata/Wikipedia content, book bodies, biography, teaching relationships, publication roles, kinbaku lineage, or claims that an authority record does not make.

Create a source-attributed digest of general tree-risk concepts: failure/defect/hazard/target distinctions; inspection context and frequency; cracks, dead wood or tops, decay and cankers, root damage or disease, recent uncompensated lean versus corrected lean, poor architecture, multiple defects, target exposure, soil disturbance or compaction, erosion, fire, flooding or saturation, construction and traffic, root lifting, soil cracks or mounding, monitoring, weather, uncertainty, and referral to a certified arborist where expertise is absent. Preserve the Region 5/California scope and the explicit statement that no inspection predicts all failures or eliminates risk. Exclude administrative forms, mitigation or cutting instructions, species/disease detail unless independently useful, and unreviewed images or third-party material. Do not turn the report's rating system into a universal score, certify any tree, branch, sling, anchor, hardpoint, suspension load, or bondage setup, or imply that tree health establishes anchor capacity; require both qualified arboricultural assessment and qualified rigging/structural judgment.

Capture only the CC BY 4.0 JATS through the EMBL-EBI Europe PMC REST endpoint and bind PMCID PMC7382548, PMID 32766466, and DOI 10.1097/PR9.0000000000000829. Preserve review design, evidence type, distinctions between preclinical models and human entrapment, heterogeneity of mechanisms and presentation, ischemia, edema and blood-nerve barrier change, fibrosis, demyelination and nodal change, axonal transport, neuroinflammation, central mechanisms, symptom-versus-sign-versus-diagnosis distinctions, test limitations, and uncertainty. Explain that the paper primarily concerns common clinical entrapment disorders, not rope bondage. Do not convert animal or disease pressure observations into safe rope pressure, placement, duration, release timing, recovery, prognosis, or treatment advice; exclude medication and procedural management as self-care instruction, and direct suspected rope-related neurologic symptoms to qualified medical evaluation.
